State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission,
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

February 25, 1985


Eric Lauterer
City Attorney
City of Garden Grove
P.O. Box 3070
Garden Grove, CA 92642

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-85-005

Dear Mr. Lauterer:

You wrote a letter January 2, 1985, requesting advice on
behalf of Councilman Milton Krieger and Planning Commissioner
Manuel Naves. This letter was received in this office on
January 7, 1985. On January 22, 1985, I wrote to you seeking
additional facts material to your advice request. On
February 7, 1985, I had my law clerk, Montessa Holt, contact
your office regarding receipt of those additional facts. To
date, we have not received that information. Therefore, at this
time we are closing our file on this advice request for
administrative reasons.

This does not preclude you from renewing your request once
you have all of the necessary information. We will, however,
treat it as a new advice request.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660           322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

January 22, 1985


Eric Lauterer
City Attorney
City of Garden Grove
P.O. Box 3070
Garden Grove, CA  92642

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our No. A-85-005

Dear Mr. Lauterer:

     You have written requesting the Commission's advice
regarding Councilmember Milton Krieger and Planning Commissioner
Manuel Nunes.  Before we can provide you with advice which is
tailored to their circumstances, we will need additional factual
information.  However, in the interim I am providing you with
copies of two previous advice letters which are relevant to
determining the issues which you have raised in your letter.

     Before we can more fully respond to your advice request, it
is important for us to know more about the "professional
connection" of Mr. Nunes with Ballew and Associates, Inc.  Is he
a part owner; if so, how much does he own? In the attached
letter from Ballew to Krieger, what is meant by the phrase
"... we propose to pay you a fee for any project which results
from your efforts or introduction.

     Your prompt response will assist us in providing you with
timely advice.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Robert E. Ledigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

RE:plh
Enclosures: Elam, No. A-82-054
            Stein, No. A-83-230

CITY OF GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA
11391 ACACIA PARKWAY, GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92640

MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. BOX 3070, GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
(714) 638-6881


February 27, 1985


Robert E. Leidigh, Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Your No. A-85-005
     Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

     In response to your letter of January 22, 1985, regarding the above
topic, I offer the following information:

     I have discussed with Councilman Krieger the letter from Ballew and
Associates to Mr. Krieger and, in particular, the phrase "...we propose to pay
you a fee for any project which results from your efforts or introduction."
As you are aware, Mr. Krieger has just started his consulting services as a
business venture within the past few months and specific dollar arrangements
have not been placed in writing between the architectural firm and Mr.
Krieger. It is his understanding that he will operate generally as follows:

     First, he will operate his own business providing governmental services
and advice to businesses primarily within the County of Orange who wish to do
development business within cities in the County, excluding the City of Garden
Grove, in which he serves as an elected official and councilmember. In
addition, he would be retained as a Director of Governmental Affairs for the
Ballew architectural firm on a retainer basis which would provide him
tentatively three percent (3%) of all business he brings to the firm up to
$100,000 and with a scale above that undecided presently between the parties.

     I reiterate that Councilman Krieger would not receive any fees or
compensation for any work performed by Ballew within the city limits of Garden
Grove or for projects to be built within the City of Garden Grove nor would he
have any other kinds of compensation from the Ballew firm for bringing to that
firm any architectural work regarding projects within the City of Garden

CITY OF GARDEN GROVE,CALIFORNIA
11391 ACACIA PARKWAY, P.O. BOX 3070, GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
(714) 638-6881

January 2, 1984


Rodger Brown
Chief Enforcement Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Request for Conflicts of Interest Opinion.

Dear Mr. Brown:

Background:

The City of Garden Grove is a general law city which has a five-member
city council with four members elected for four year terms and one mayor
elected for a two-year term. Milton Krieger has been on the Council for over
ten years and at the recent November election was re-elected for four more
years. He is presently in the process of changing his occupational area from
being involved in selling retail and wholesale furniture products to that of a
consultant in the general area of intergovernmental relations. Councilman
Krieger having acquire over the years, substantial areas of expertise in
connection with the function of local government including the land issues
that are concerned therewith, has decided to place that expertise in an area
that will allow him to operate as a consultant dealing with communities
primarily in Southern California and developers and property thereof.

In connection therewith he contemplates entering into a retainer
agreement with Ballew and Associates Inc. located in Orange County (the county
in which the City of Garden Grove is located) under the terms of which he will
receive compensation under a formula in accordance with the attached letter.

The City of Garden Grove has a planning agency separate of the City
Council and the members thereto are appointed by the Mayor with the consent of
the City Council. Mr. Manuel Nunes presently serves on the Planning
Commission since June 18, 1984. He was recommended to the Mayor by Councilman
Milton Krieger. By profession he is an architect with offices located in the
County of Orange and professionally connected with Ballew and Associates, Inc.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page Two                                                    1/2/85


Question:

     Because of the importance of both Councilman Krieger as a voting member
of the City Council and Planning Commissioner Manual Nunes as one of the seven
members of the City's Planning Commission, it is critical that no impediments
be created which would prevent them from participating in any proceedings
coming before the public bodies.

     Because of the financial arrangements described above, it appears that
under existing law and, in particular, the conflict of interest provisions
related to the Political Reform Act, that circumstances are now present that
may place them in a position of non-participation and having to declare in a
public meeting a conflict of interest because of the financial interest they
may have.

     Two categories appear to exist - one is those matters which would come
before the bodies which are part of the activities in the City of Garden
Grove, that is, circumstances wherein either Councilman Krieger as a
consultant received compensation in connection with a project that ultimately
comes before the City Council or the situation where Commissioner Nunes is
asked to review, as a Planning Commissioner, the matter prior to it reaching
the Council.  To date one specific factual pattern in this area exists.

     For the past two years Councilman Krieger has also served as Chairman
of the Garden Grove Agency for Community Development (a redevelopment agency
created under state law).  In that capacity and in the capacity as a council
member and in the capacity as a subcommittee chairman dealing with economic
development in the community, he along with other council members have spoken
to potential developers who might develop parcels of land owned by the Agency
in the community.  In particular, one company was Webb Company.  The Company
initially was brought to the attention of Councilman Krieger by former City
employee, Mannie Nunes, now Commissioner Nunes.

     Thereafter it appeared they were interested in developing a particular
parcel of eight acres in the community which had been discussed.  Their
interest in development of that parcel was brought to light and Councilman
Krieger, along with a City staff member and with the Council's knowledge and
approval, met with the developer, Webb Company, at their home office in
Lexington, Kentucky.  As a result of those discussions, the developer has
indicated an interest to enter into negotiations with the Garden Grove Agency
for Community Development as to a particular parcel of land in the community.
The developer has indicated that they want to retain the architectural firm of
Planning Commissioner Manual Nunes to provide a portion of the services in
connection with the project.  In addition, it is that same firm with which
Councilman Krieger, after the first of the year, has indicated his intention
to be retained as a consultant on a retainer formula.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page Three                                                1/2/85

     I am advised by both Councilman Krieger and Planning Commissioner Nunes
that neither party contemplates receiving any compensation from any company
that because of city business will have a matter brought before the Planning
Commission or City Council.

     Based on the preceding information and any further details that you
also may desire, I am requesting an opinion as to the areas described.

                                        Respectfully,

                                        [signature]
                                        ERIC LAUTERER
                                        City Attorney

EL:mes
5028G
cc: City Manager
    Attachment

BALLEW
AND
ASSOCIATES
INC.


December 14, 1984


Mr. Milton Krieger
12761 Canter Street
Garden Grove, Ca. 92645

Dear Milt,

This is to cover our recent conversations regarding your
upcoming affiliation to our company as Business Development/
Governmental Relations Consultant.

We are excited about this experiment, whose approach you
and ourselves will review after six months, and look forward
to working with you, officially beginning January 1, 1985,
if that is acceptable.

The method of operation is flexible from our viewpoint,
and we do not at this time have a plan on how this should
all happen. Your views and ideas on the subject are welcome.

Essentially, our perception of this relationship is one
where you assist us in the promotion of new business through
your network of associates in the private and government
sectors. We will collaborate with you on project leads
which you may uncover and generally supply you with logis-
tical support, as necessary.

To compensate you for your efforts, we propose to pay you
a $ 500.00 per month consultation fee, the first payment
due on January 15, 1985. Additionally, we propose to pay
you a fee for any project which results from your efforts
or introduction. The amount of this, we suggest, should
be a percentage of our gross contract sum, worked per the
following formula, to be prorated and paid to you upon our

Ballew & Associa tes, Inc., Michael B. Weidman, Architect
Planning/Architectu...
24853 Del Prado, Lina Point, California 92629
(714) 240-1936 / (-4) 831-3274

receipt of payment from contractual agreements:

$ 0 - $ 100,000. contract - 3%
$ 100,000. -$ 250,000. - 2.75%
Over $. 250,000. - 2.5%

Please examine the preceeding and let us know whether
you feel it is acceptable. For our part, we have not
attempted a scheme such as this in the past, and will
work with any plan that is equitable.

I am enthusiastic about the potential of our association,
and anticipate much success for us all. More importantly,
we hope you will be happy working with us and that this .will
become the first step into a successful land and building
development career for you.

Best Wishes,

John J. Ballew

JJB/bb

Robert E. Leidigh
Page Two                                                    2/27/85


Grove.  He would not be a stockholder in the firm, nor a partner in the firm,
nor entitled to division of any profits in the firm, which could in any way
compensate him for work performed within the City limits of Garden Grove by
the Ballew architectural firm.

     Drawing your attention to the status of Mr. Manuel Nunes with Ballew
and Associates, I am advised that as of February 26, 1985, he has no written
contractual arrangement of ownership with the company.  I s. Ballew and
Associates.  He presently is employed on a fixed salary with certain company
benefits including health insurance and an automobile.  Ballew and Associates
are in the process of having their attorneys and their accountants compute the
value of the company in order to compute a formula that is agreeable to both
Ballew and Associates and Mr. Nunes as to ownership rights for Mr. Nunes.

     Though verbal discussions have gone on between Mr. Nunes and Ballew and
Associates, he presently is not a part owner of the company and, in fact, owns
no portion of the company.


     I trust this will provide sufficient information so that you can
proceed with our request.

                                        Respectfully yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        ERIC LAUTERER
                                        City Attorney
